situation: audit-2026-07-08-043 finding AE-L-001: protocol-check.py:72 subprocess.run inherits full parent os.environ (including ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, etc.) when invoking bootstrap.py. Partial fix 09374bb added --project-dir flag but never narrowed env. Design invites future secret leak if bootstrap.py or any transitive import reads os.environ.
action: Replace {**os.environ, 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR': WORKSPACE_ROOT} with explicit whitelist (PATH, PYTHONPATH, HOME, TMPDIR, TEMP, TMP, LANG, LC_ALL) + CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR. Whitelist covers standard subprocess needs without exposing secrets.
outcome_evidence: Commit 0e0a58a on auto/opt-protocol-check-1783389515 branch; worktree at /home/admin/workspace/.opt-worktrees/autonomous-engine/opt-protocol-check-1783389515 contains whitelisted env dict at lines 68-77 replacing prior os.environ spread.
next_suggestion: Dispatch AE-L-002 (notify-phone.py ntfy body unbounded truncation). After both merged, set audit-cycle-state.status=cycle-complete to trigger next full audit.

audit_findings.finding: subprocess.run env={**os.environ, ...} inherits full parent environment including secrets (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, SSH_AUTH_SOCK, etc.). Current bootstrap.py path doesn't exist so risk is latent, but design invites future leak if bootstrap script is installed or subprocess target changes.
audit_findings.remediation: Whitelist only necessary env vars (PATH/PYTHONPATH/HOME/TMP*/LANG/LC_ALL) plus CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR.
